body.gate: gates on T-devlab-schema-create (devlab schema must exist before constraints table is written)
body.title: [sprint] Constraint normalizer: unify design rules + completion criteria into queryable schema
body.intention: I intend that constraints from palace rules, design rules, safeguards, and completion criteria are normalized into a single schema other agents can reason against without re-parsing prose.
body.description:
Currently constraints live in multiple places in different formats: palace rules (prose), ticket design rules (field), completion criteria (field), safeguards (palace node), CLAUDE.md hard rules. No agent can reason uniformly across all of them.

A constraint normalizer agent ingests all constraint sources and produces a uniform schema (e.g. {id, source, constraint_text, domain, severity}). Downstream agents (judge, scenario generator) query this schema rather than knowing about all the sources.

This is the load-bearing missing piece between IBD ticket intentions and compiled-inference test generation. Observed in working form at a parallel project (test automation context).

**Affected files:** TBD — new agent, likely devices/hubert/ or new devices/normalizer/
**Design rules:** external-state (schema persisted, not in-memory)
**Scope boundary:** IN — ingest + normalize constraints into schema. OUT — using the schema (that's judge + scenario-generator tickets).
**Completion criteria:** Constraint normalizer produces a queryable list from at least 3 sources (palace rules, ticket completion criteria, safeguards); downstream query returns matching constraints by domain/severity.
